headline: ELDORADO GOLD CLOSES $3.8B Foran Mining Deal, Adding Saskatchewan Copper-Zinc-Gold Asset
snippet: Eldorado Gold (TSX: ELD) completed its $3.8 billion acquisition of Foran Mining on April 14, adding the McIlvenna Bay copper-zinc-gold-silver deposit in Saskatchewan's Flin Flon Greenstone Belt. The deal gives Eldorado critical mineral exposure and exploration upside in a tier-one jurisdiction, with Foran now delisting from the TSX.

headline: CHINA HIKES Rare Earth Prices 44% for Q2 — Baotou Steel Surges 8.5%
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth set its Q2 2026 concentrate price at 38,804 yuan/tonne — a 44.6% jump over Q1 and the seventh consecutive quarterly increase. Baotou Steel surged 8.5% on the news; the hike reshapes cost assumptions for downstream magnet and EV manufacturers globally and strengthens the case for non-Chinese rare earth projects.

headline: SILVER AT $72: White Metal Outshines Gold as AI and Solar Ignite Industrial Squeeze
snippet: Silver hit $72/oz on April 14 as the gold-silver ratio compressed to 64:1 — down from 90:1 two years ago. AI data centers now consume 2-3x more silver per unit than traditional hardware, solar absorbs ~200 million oz annually, and 70% of silver is mined as a byproduct, meaning supply cannot quickly respond to the structural sixth-year deficit.

headline: GOLD'S HISTORIC FLIGHT: $4,800 Level Signals Deepening Anxiety Over U.S. Fiscal Stability
snippet: Gold climbed to $4,800/oz — 44% above year-ago levels — as U.S. debt crosses $39 trillion with monthly interest payments rivalling the defence budget. The April analysis argues this is a structural portfolio shift, not a speculative spike, with institutions treating gold as a core holding as Treasuries lose credibility with large allocators.

headline: K92 MINING Q1 2026: 46,743 oz AuEq at 10.2 g/t Head Grade, Stage 3 Expansion on Track
snippet: K92 Mining (TSX: KNT) produced 46,743 oz AuEq at its Kainantu Gold Mine in Q1 2026, including 44,022 oz gold at a 10.2 g/t head grade with ore throughput up 37% year-over-year. Record quarterly lateral development of 3,007 metres now exceeds Stage 3 expansion requirements; 2026 guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq reaffirmed with H2 weighted.

headline: ABRASILVER HITS 171 METRES at 0.83 g/t Gold at Diablillos Oculto East, Phase VI Launched
snippet: AbraSilver Resource (TSX-V: ABRA) drilled 171m at 0.83 g/t gold — including 9m at 3.46 g/t — at the Oculto East target of its Diablillos project in Argentina while launching a 15,000m Phase VI drill program. Results confirm gold-dominant mineralization extending several hundred metres east of the current open-pit resource boundary.

headline: COPPER RETREATS to $5.80/lb as LME Stockpiles Hit 8-Year High — Tariff Decision Looms
snippet: Copper eased to $5.80/lb from January's record $6.01/lb as LME warehouse inventory climbed to an eight-year high, signalling near-term demand softness. A U.S. tariff decision is due by June 30 — a potential 15% duty on refined copper effective January 2027 rising to 30% in 2028 — continuing to distort physical flows and junior copper project valuations.

headline: MAINSTREAM ECONOMISTS vs. GOLD: 100 Years of Wrong Predictions — and Gold Is Still Winning
snippet: Money Metals' Mike Maharrey traces the century-long fight between establishment economics and gold — from Keynes calling it a 'barbarous relic' in 1923 to today's $4,800 spot price and record U.S. monthly deficits. Published April 14, the piece argues every generation of economists has been wrong on the same trade while gold holders have been quietly right.

headline: BULL TREND BROADENS: Aluminum Breaks Out, Drags Gold, Silver, and Base Metals Higher
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian Metals Market Update notes an unusually broad rally across the metals complex, with aluminum posting an outsized surge before copper, nickel, and zinc followed. Gold and silver are holding technical breakouts simultaneously — a breadth signal not seen across precious and base metals since early 2024.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ TONNES of Silver in Early 2026 — Sixth Consecutive Supply Deficit Confirmed
snippet: China absorbed over 790 tonnes of silver in the first two months of 2026 — an eight-year import high — with February alone at nearly 470 tonnes as solar manufacturers front-loaded ahead of VAT rebate cuts. The Silver Institute projects a 67-million-ounce structural shortfall in 2026, confirming the sixth consecutive annual supply deficit.
